check: Server-side tagging (sGTM or platform-native CAPI/Events API) implemented for at least the top revenue-driving platforms
description: Server-side tag management or a platform-native server-side API (Meta CAPI, TikTok Events API, Google Enhanced Conversions) is in place for the platforms receiving the largest ad spend allocation
why_it_matters: This is the single highest-leverage fix for tracking accuracy in the current privacy-restricted browser environment — prioritize the platforms with the most spend/decision-weight first if implementing incrementally

check: Server-side hit ratio monitored as a health metric, not assumed complete once implemented
description: The proportion of events successfully received server-side (vs. lost entirely) is monitored on an ongoing basis, since server-side implementations can silently degrade (a broken deployment, an expired token) without an obvious symptom
why_it_matters: An initially-correct server-side setup can silently break and no one notices until someone investigates a conversion-tracking discrepancy — proactive monitoring catches this before it distorts weeks of optimization decisions

check: Every platform with both browser and server-side tracking has verified event deduplication
description: For every platform running dual browser+server tracking (Meta, TikTok, Google), deduplication (matching event_id or equivalent) is verified to actually work, not just assumed configured correctly
why_it_matters: Undetected deduplication failure inflates reported conversions, which can look like a tracking win while actually corrupting every optimization and reporting decision downstream — this needs active verification, not launch-and-forget

check: Personal data (email, phone) hashed before transmission to any ad platform, never sent in plaintext
description: Enhanced Conversions, CAPI, and Events API payloads hash PII (SHA-256, per each platform's specification) client- or server-side before transmission, never sending raw email/phone
why_it_matters: This is both a platform requirement (payloads with unhashed PII are typically rejected or non-compliant) and a genuine privacy/security obligation — this is the ads-specific application of general PII-handling discipline from the Security module

check: Consent Mode (or platform equivalent) implemented and correctly reflects actual user consent state
description: Google's Consent Mode v2 (or the equivalent mechanism for other platforms) is implemented so tracking behavior genuinely adjusts based on the user's actual cookie/tracking consent choice, not left in a default-allow or misconfigured state
why_it_matters: Consent Mode misconfiguration is both a compliance risk (GDPR/ePrivacy and similar regulations) and, separately, actively degrades data quality if consent signals aren't being correctly respected and modeled

check: Consent Mode's modeled conversions understood and factored into performance interpretation, not treated as directly-observed data
description: Where Consent Mode's conversion modeling (statistical estimation for users who didn't consent) is active, this is understood as modeled rather than directly observed data when interpreting campaign performance
why_it_matters: Treating modeled conversions with the same confidence as directly observed ones can lead to over-confident optimization decisions based on statistically estimated, not measured, outcomes

check: Attribution model choice matches the actual customer journey length and complexity, not left at platform default without review
description: The attribution model used for reporting/optimization (data-driven, last-click, position-based) is deliberately chosen based on the account's actual typical path-to-conversion, not left unreviewed at whatever the platform defaulted to
why_it_matters: A short, single-touch-typical customer journey and a long, multi-touch B2B journey need different attribution approaches to fairly credit what's actually driving conversions — a mismatched model can misattribute credit across channels/campaigns

check: Data-Driven Attribution used where available and sufficient conversion volume exists to support it
description: Google's Data-Driven Attribution (or equivalent statistical model) is used over simpler rule-based models when the account has enough conversion volume for it to be statistically meaningful
why_it_matters: DDA generally produces more accurate multi-touch credit distribution than fixed rule-based models, but needs sufficient data volume to be reliable — check both availability and adequacy of underlying data before assuming it's the right default

check: A cross-platform view of the customer journey exists (even approximate), not each platform's siloed self-reported attribution taken at face value
description: Some mechanism (a marketing mix model, a unified analytics platform, or at minimum a directional understanding) exists for reconciling the fact that Google, Meta, and TikTok will each self-report attribution in ways that, summed together, typically overstate total conversions
why_it_matters: Every ad platform's attribution is inherently biased toward crediting itself — taking each platform's self-reported numbers at face value and summing them across platforms systematically overstates total marketing-driven conversions

check: MER (Marketing Efficiency Ratio, total revenue / total spend) tracked as a sanity check against platform-reported ROAS
description: Blended MER is calculated and tracked alongside individual platform ROAS figures as an independent sanity check that isn't subject to any single platform's attribution bias
why_it_matters: When platform-reported ROAS collectively implies more revenue than the business actually generated, MER is the ground-truth check that catches this — a discipline every account running multiple ad platforms should maintain

check: AdAttributionKit or platform-equivalent implemented for iOS app advertisers
description: For mobile app advertisers specifically, AdAttributionKit (view-through attribution with a defined post-impression window) is implemented to recover iOS attribution signal lost to ATT/App Tracking Transparency restrictions
why_it_matters: iOS privacy restrictions materially degraded traditional mobile attribution — AdAttributionKit and equivalent frameworks are the current mechanism for recovering enough signal to optimize mobile app campaigns effectively

check: Mobile Measurement Partner (AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Singular) correctly integrated for app advertisers running multiple ad networks
description: App advertisers running campaigns across multiple ad networks use an MMP for unified, network-agnostic attribution rather than relying solely on each network's own siloed attribution reporting
why_it_matters: Without an MMP, an app advertiser faces the same cross-platform attribution bias problem as `ADS059`, specific to mobile — each network's SDK-based attribution will tend to over-credit itself absent a neutral third-party reconciliation layer

check: UTM parameters used consistently across all campaigns for downstream analytics reconciliation
description: Every ad platform's campaigns use a consistent UTM tagging convention so downstream analytics (GA4 or equivalent) can reconcile traffic sources accurately against ad-platform-reported data
why_it_matters: Inconsistent or missing UTM tagging breaks the ability to reconcile ad-platform data against an independent analytics source — this undermines exactly the cross-platform sanity-checking that `ADS059`/`ADS060` depend on

check: Attribution/conversion windows configured deliberately per platform and business type, not left at platform defaults universally
description: Click-through and view-through attribution windows are reviewed and set based on the account's actual typical consideration period, not left at whatever each platform defaults to universally across all account types
why_it_matters: A short attribution window on a long-consideration-cycle B2B product undercounts real conversions; a long window on an impulse-purchase product over-credits ads for conversions that would have happened anyway — this should be a deliberate match to the actual buying behavior
